name: (a) h2h NET — clean win. Subject A (Rick) total net 70 beats Subject B (Ben) 73; Ben's stakeholder pays Rick's.
_contract: Golden HAND-CALC fixture for the NEW bets engine (engine/bets/). The engine CONSUMES net-per-hole as a GIVEN input (P2 — it never re-derives net; netForSegment is validated separately by a net-reconciliation test). The `expected` block is hand-calculated and is the source of truth the engine must match. SettlementEdge direction convention: fromPlayerId PAYS toPlayerId (debtor -> creditor), matching the project's 'X->Y $N' settle-up convention. Money in integer cents.
_handCalc: Rick net total = 35 (front: 4+4+3+4+5+4+4+3+4) + 35 (back: 4+4+4+3+4+4+4+4+4) = 70. Ben net total = 38 (front: 4+5+4+4+4+4+5+4+4) + 35 (back: 4+4+4+4+4+4+4+4+3) = 73. Lower total wins: 70 < 73, so subject A (Rick) wins by a margin of 3. h2h is winner-take-the-stake (not per-stroke): the LOSER's stakeholder (Ben) pays the WINNER's stakeholder (Rick) the full $20.00 = 2000 cents. Exactly one SettlementEdge: ben -> rick, 2000c.
